text:
In the enchanted land of Farland, there lived an elven bard named Lyra who sang songs only birds could hear. Once upon a sunny day, while lounging on a branch, Lyra heard the most charming melody. Peering around the tree branch, she saw a strange little bird she hadn time hadn't noticed before - a wood pewernet singing a love song to a particular flowering bush.

Curiosity pounced, Lyra flew down to listen closer. The pewney bird called out, and Lyra flew closer. It sung about love in springtime blooming petals. As it sang, the bush blossomed out into a lovely bouquet full of bees humming happily.

Lyra had to admire the wood pewny's song. She swooped down on the time-machine she wore as jewelry. With a hop, skip, and a pewny song, she went back into the time machine. She'd remembered this charming melody years ago.
